displayTitle: Two Properties the Part Won't Let You Choose

beats.narration: Same probe, same frequency, different alloy, completely different signal. Two material properties you don't control do most of the talking.

payload.title: CONDUCTIVITY & PERMEABILITY
payload.sub: The two knobs the part owns, not you.

beats.narration: Conductivity is graded on the IACS scale. Copper defines one hundred percent. Aerospace aluminum lands near thirty. Titanium and stainless limp in near three.
Raise conductivity and eddy currents grow stronger, but penetration shrinks. Copper at one hundred kilohertz tests barely two tenths of a millimeter deep.

payload.heading: What conductivity does to the signal

chips.s: Operating point travels the comma curve
σ sits in the depth denominator

beats.narration: Permeability is the second property, and when it appears, it wins. Ferromagnetic steel multiplies the coil's flux and collapses penetration to a thin skin.

payload.heading: When permeability shows up, it wins
payload.note: FERROMAGNETIC: μ MULTIPLIES FLUX → REACTANCE CLIMBS, DEPTH COLLAPSES

beats.narration: The cure is magnetic saturation, forcing steel to act nonmagnetic. Master both properties to sort alloys, verify heat treatment, and catch hidden fire damage.

payload.sub: Know which regime you're in before you trust a number.
